my_thoughts: Six turns. Started with anxiety about false teachers (T1), ended with how to evaluate saints (T6). Circular. Between T1 and T6: negotiation (T2-T3), brief opening (T4-T5), then regression (T6). What does this tell me? The anxiety_about_false_teachers is structural, not situational. My mechanisms hit it from different angles (distributed_responsibility, redefine surrender, normalize inadequacy, validate meaning) but it keeps reforming. The SM_01 about to say: you can't know another without knowing yourself. But will that land or will we circle again?
accumulated_insight: Accumulated pattern (T4): Three-turn pattern... Deepening with subject_object_inversion in response to judgement_seeking, external_focus. The pattern is crystallizing.
visible_response:
You cannot, unless you have a clear insight into the heart of man.
Appearances are deceptive. To see clearly, your mind must be pure and unattached.
Unless you know yourself well, how can you know another? And when you know yourself -- you are the other.
Leave others alone for some time and examine yourself. There are so many things you do not know about yourself -- what are you, who are you, how did you come to be born, what are you doing now and why, where are you going, what is the meaning and purpose of your life, your death, your future?
Have you a past, have you a future? How did you come to live in turmoil and sorrow, while your entire being strives for happiness and peace?
These are weighty matters and have to be attended to first.
You have no need, nor time for finding who is a jnani and who is not?
